subject.label: Draftsman UI Taxonomy
subject.summary: Meta review subject for the Draftsman shell itself. Each route maps a UI region, panel, object, behavior, or code reference into a reviewable focus panel.

routes.label: Draftsman UI
routes.summary: Root route for reviewing the clean Draftsman QML shell.
routes.purpose: Give the human one navigable map of every UI region, panel, object, behavior, and code reference.
prompts: Does the shell read as a draftsman table?
Can the human find each region quickly?
Is the UI quiet enough for long review sessions?

routes.label: Top Chrome
routes.summary: Reserved top-level app identity and global command area.
routes.purpose: Define what global chrome should contain before it is added.
prompts: Do we need a top bar, or is the status bar enough?
Should global actions be icons only?

routes.label: App Title
routes.summary: Name of the current shell or project.
routes.purpose: Keep the app identity visible without becoming a marketing header.
prompts: Should title live in top chrome or status bar?
Does it waste vertical space?

routes.label: Location Label
routes.summary: Human-readable path to the selected review route.
routes.purpose: Make current context obvious when the user drills deep.
prompts: Is the breadcrumb enough?
Should this also show project name?

routes.label: Shell Toggles
routes.summary: Future show/hide controls for major panels.
routes.purpose: Let the human control density later without losing the base layout.
prompts: Are toggles needed now?
Should panel visibility persist?

routes.label: Activity Rail
routes.summary: Compact global mode switcher on the far left.
routes.purpose: Provide stable mode navigation without taking over project navigation.
prompts: Are labels/icons clear?
Should the rail be hideable?
Does selected state read at a glance?

routes.label: Binder Mode
routes.summary: Future project binder mode.
routes.purpose: Reserve navigation for binder/project organization.
prompts: Should binder be default later?
What opens in center when selected?

routes.label: Review Mode
routes.summary: Current UI taxonomy review gate mode.
routes.purpose: Open review routes and comments.
prompts: Is review mode clearly different from binder mode?

routes.label: Settings Mode
routes.summary: Future layout/theme/settings mode.
routes.purpose: Reserve a top-level settings entry.
prompts: Should settings also be in the left panel?

routes.label: Proof Mode
routes.summary: Future proof screenshot and receipt view.
routes.purpose: Keep proof visible as a first-class workflow.
prompts: Should proof be a shelf or a mode?

routes.label: Left Panel
routes.summary: Project, workflow, and review-subject navigation.
routes.purpose: Make project organization permanent and keep review routes easy to enter.
prompts: Is this project organization or just placeholder rows?
Should each section collapse?
Are route counts useful?

routes.label: Project Navigator
routes.summary: Project slots and future project list.
routes.purpose: Select the active project/context.
prompts: Should project slots come from JSON?
Should scratch/final be user renameable?

routes.label: Workflow Navigator
routes.summary: Workflow buckets for planning, review, proof, and output.
routes.purpose: Keep the human oriented during repeated work.
prompts: Which workflow states are real?
Should this mirror project config?

routes.label: Review Subjects
routes.summary: Entry point for UI taxonomy and future review subjects.
routes.purpose: Select the active review subject without leaving the shell.
prompts: How many subjects can this list handle?
Should subjects show note/status counts?

routes.label: Settings Entry
routes.summary: Secondary settings entry inside project navigation.
routes.purpose: Provide discoverable settings without relying only on activity rail.
prompts: Duplicate with activity rail or useful?
Should it open a settings route?

routes.label: Main Workspace
routes.summary: Primary route review surface.
routes.purpose: Show the selected route as a focus panel with child routes, notes, code refs, and prompts.
prompts: Is one selected route clearly dominant?
Are cards useful without becoming clutter?

routes.label: Workspace Header
routes.summary: Current subject and route summary.
routes.purpose: Make context clear before the focus content.
prompts: Is the header too large?
Does it repeat the status bar?

routes.label: Breadcrumb
routes.summary: Route path from root to current focus.
routes.purpose: Let deep review routes stay understandable.
prompts: Should breadcrumb segments be clickable?
Does it wrap cleanly?

routes.label: Focus Panel
routes.summary: Main selected route details.
routes.purpose: Give the human one route/object to review at a time.
prompts: Is the review prompt direct enough?
Should code refs live here or right panel only?

routes.label: Route Cards
routes.summary: Drilldown cards for child routes.
routes.purpose: Let the human browse one compression layer at a time.
prompts: Are cards scannable?
Should they show code refs or keep that for the inspector?

routes.label: Comment Thread
routes.summary: In-memory human notes attached to this exact route.
routes.purpose: Let the human leave precise feedback without persistence risk.
prompts: Is in-memory clearly marked?
What should the eventual save contract require?

routes.label: Right Panel
routes.summary: Context inspector for selected route and object facts.
routes.purpose: Show route facts, code refs, notes, and receipts without duplicating the whole center page.
prompts: Does it follow the selected center route?
What belongs here versus the bottom shelf?

routes.label: Route Facts
routes.summary: Current route id, type, status, and child count.
routes.purpose: Expose structured facts for review and future agent reads.
prompts: Are facts enough for an agent handoff?

routes.label: Code References
routes.summary: Source paths tied to the selected route.
routes.purpose: Connect UI review notes to the actual files humans or agents should inspect.
prompts: Should code refs validate file existence?
Should click open Qt Creator?

routes.label: Notes Preview
routes.summary: Recent notes for the selected route.
routes.purpose: Keep human feedback visible while browsing.
prompts: Should this preview all route notes or only the latest?

routes.label: Write Boundary
routes.summary: Explicit notice that this pass does not write files.
routes.purpose: Prevent accidental persistence while the UI model is still forming.
prompts: What must be true before writes are allowed?

routes.label: Bottom Panel
routes.summary: Secondary shelf for output, proof, receipts, and logs.
routes.purpose: Show session feedback without stealing focus from the main route.
prompts: Should this be collapsible?
Which feature owns each shelf?

routes.label: Output Shelf
routes.summary: Current session output.
routes.purpose: Show low-priority operational feedback.
prompts: Is this useful enough to keep visible?

routes.label: Proof Shelf
routes.summary: Future screenshot/proof output.
routes.purpose: Reserve proof as a visible workflow.
prompts: Should proof be stored by route?

routes.label: Receipt Shelf
routes.summary: Future write/action receipts.
routes.purpose: Show what changed and who/what did it.
prompts: What should a receipt contain?

routes.label: Log Shelf
routes.summary: Future runtime log view.
routes.purpose: Expose app events without cluttering right context.
prompts: Should log persist?

routes.label: Status Bar
routes.summary: Always-visible session state.
routes.purpose: Show current mode, subject, route, note count, and write status.
prompts: Is this too dense?
Does it help recover context?

routes.label: Settings
routes.summary: Future human customization surfaces.
routes.purpose: Reserve theme, layout, feature, and project settings.
prompts: What settings are mandatory before persistence?
Should settings be a route?

routes.label: Theme Settings
routes.summary: Low-glare palette and typography controls.
routes.purpose: Let the human control base, surface, accent, text, and fonts.
prompts: Should colors be editable in-app now?
Are current colors eye-safe?

routes.label: Layout Settings
routes.summary: Panel widths, shelf height, and visibility.
routes.purpose: Make the shell customizable later.
prompts: Should splitters write settings?
What is the default compact layout?

routes.label: Feature Settings
routes.summary: Enable/disable project-specific workspaces.
routes.purpose: Keep custom UIs modular.
prompts: Should feature settings exist before multiple features?

routes.label: Write Rules
routes.summary: No file writes until approved.
routes.purpose: Protect project data while the UI foundation is forming.
prompts: What exact file should notes save to?
Should each save produce a receipt?

routes.label: Feature Surfaces
routes.summary: Reusable custom workspaces hosted by the shell.
routes.purpose: Keep project-specific UI separate from core regions.
prompts: Does feature code stay isolated?
How does a feature target a region?

routes.label: UI Taxonomy Feature
routes.summary: The first meta review feature.
routes.purpose: Review this shell panel by panel.
prompts: Is this enough to review the UI itself?

routes.label: Review Gate Feature
routes.summary: Generic route-attached review pattern.
routes.purpose: Reuse the review gate for non-UI artifacts later.
prompts: What parts should be generic versus UI taxonomy-specific?

routes.label: Text Editor Feature
routes.summary: Future local scratch/text review feature.
routes.purpose: Reserve the concept without importing old C++ code.
prompts: Do we need this in QML now?

routes.label: Data Contracts
routes.summary: Human/agent editable JSON contracts.
routes.purpose: Separate app behavior from project-specific data.
prompts: Which contracts need schemas?
Which contracts can agents fill?

routes.label: Proof / Review
routes.summary: Visual proof and review receipts.
routes.purpose: Capture evidence for UI changes.
prompts: What proof is enough for a UI change?
Should proof be generated from CMake?
